antelope.description: A fast hoofed mammal known for agility, alert behavior, and slender horns in many species.
antelope.habitat: Grasslands, savannas, shrublands, and open woodlands.
antelope.diet: Herbivore: grasses, leaves, shoots, and herbs.
antelope.fun_fact: Many antelopes rely on speed and sharp senses to avoid predators.

badger.description: A sturdy burrowing mammal with strong claws and a reputation for being defensive when threatened.
badger.habitat: Woodlands, grasslands, farmland edges, and underground burrows.
badger.diet: Omnivore: insects, worms, small animals, roots, fruit, and eggs.
badger.fun_fact: Badgers can dig complex tunnel systems called setts.

bat.description: The only mammal capable of true sustained flight, with many species using echolocation.
bat.habitat: Caves, forests, buildings, and tree hollows.
bat.diet: Mostly insects, fruit, nectar, or small animals depending on species.
bat.fun_fact: Some bats can locate tiny insects in complete darkness using sound.

bear.description: A powerful large mammal with strong limbs, keen smell, and varied behavior across species.
bear.habitat: Forests, mountains, tundra, and coastal regions.
bear.diet: Omnivore: berries, roots, fish, insects, carrion, and mammals.
bear.fun_fact: A bear's sense of smell is among the strongest in the animal kingdom.

bee.description: A pollinating insect that helps plants reproduce and often lives in highly organized colonies.
bee.habitat: Gardens, forests, fields, orchards, and hives.
bee.fun_fact: Bees communicate food locations through movement patterns known as dances.

beetle.description: A diverse insect with hardened forewings that protect the flying wings underneath.
beetle.habitat: Forests, grasslands, soil, freshwater edges, and gardens.
beetle.diet: Plants, fungi, wood, carrion, dung, or smaller insects depending on species.
beetle.fun_fact: Beetles make up one of the largest groups of animals on Earth.

bison.description: A large grazing mammal with a heavy shoulder hump, thick coat, and strong herd behavior.
bison.diet: Herbivore: grasses, sedges, and other low plants.
bison.fun_fact: Bison can run surprisingly fast despite their massive size.

boar.description: A wild pig with tusks, a strong snout, and adaptable feeding habits.
boar.habitat: Forests, wetlands, grasslands, and farmland edges.
boar.diet: Omnivore: roots, fruit, insects, eggs, small animals, and crops.
boar.fun_fact: Boars use their snouts to root through soil for food.

butterfly.description: A colorful flying insect known for delicate wings and a complete life cycle from caterpillar to adult.
butterfly.habitat: Gardens, meadows, forests, wetlands, and open fields.
butterfly.diet: Nectar as adults; leaves as caterpillars.
butterfly.fun_fact: Butterflies taste with sensors on their feet.

cat.description: A small carnivorous mammal known for stealth, balance, and close association with humans.
cat.habitat: Homes, cities, farms, and many outdoor environments.
cat.diet: Carnivore: meat-based food, small mammals, birds, and insects.
cat.fun_fact: Cats use their whiskers to judge spaces and detect nearby movement.

caterpillar.description: The larval stage of butterflies and moths, usually focused on eating and growing.
caterpillar.habitat: Leaves, stems, gardens, forests, and crop plants.
caterpillar.diet: Mostly herbivore: leaves and plant material.
caterpillar.fun_fact: A caterpillar transforms into an adult butterfly or moth through metamorphosis.

chimpanzee.description: A highly intelligent primate with complex social behavior and tool use.
chimpanzee.habitat: Tropical forests, woodland mosaics, and savannas.
chimpanzee.diet: Omnivore: fruit, leaves, seeds, insects, and small animals.
chimpanzee.fun_fact: Chimpanzees can use sticks and stones as tools.

cockroach.description: A hardy insect that can live in many environments and is often active at night.
cockroach.habitat: Leaf litter, caves, buildings, sewers, and warm damp places.
cockroach.diet: Omnivore: decaying matter, crumbs, plants, and many organic materials.
cockroach.fun_fact: Cockroaches are ancient insects that existed long before humans.

cow.description: A domesticated grazing mammal raised for milk, meat, leather, and farm work.
cow.diet: Herbivore: grass, hay, silage, and grains.
cow.fun_fact: Cows are ruminants, meaning they digest food through a multi-chambered stomach.

coyote.description: A clever wild canine known for adaptability and a wide range of vocal calls.
coyote.habitat: Grasslands, deserts, forests, suburbs, and farmland.
coyote.diet: Omnivore: small mammals, birds, fruit, insects, carrion, and scraps.
coyote.fun_fact: Coyotes can thrive close to cities by adjusting their behavior.

crab.description: A crustacean with a broad body, hard shell, claws, and sideways walking movement.
crab.habitat: Oceans, beaches, tide pools, mangroves, rivers, and estuaries.
crab.diet: Omnivore: algae, detritus, small animals, and carrion.
crab.fun_fact: Many crabs can regenerate lost legs over time.

crow.description: A highly intelligent black bird known for problem solving, memory, and social behavior.
crow.habitat: Forests, farms, towns, cities, and open landscapes.
crow.diet: Omnivore: insects, seeds, fruit, eggs, small animals, carrion, and scraps.
crow.fun_fact: Crows can remember human faces and learn from each other.

deer.description: A graceful hoofed mammal, often recognized by antlers in males of many species.
deer.habitat: Forests, grasslands, wetlands, mountains, and farmland edges.
deer.diet: Herbivore: leaves, shoots, grass, bark, fruit, and shrubs.
deer.fun_fact: Antlers are shed and regrown regularly in many deer species.

dog.description: A domesticated canine known for loyalty, scent ability, and many working roles.
dog.habitat: Homes, farms, cities, and human-managed environments.
dog.diet: Omnivore: meat-based food, grains, vegetables, and prepared dog food.
dog.fun_fact: Dogs have an extremely sensitive sense of smell.

dolphin.description: An intelligent marine mammal known for social groups, clicks, whistles, and agile swimming.
dolphin.habitat: Oceans, coastal waters, and some rivers.
dolphin.fun_fact: Dolphins use echolocation to find prey underwater.

donkey.description: A domesticated equine valued for endurance, sure footing, and calm strength.
donkey.diet: Herbivore: grasses, hay, shrubs, and rough vegetation.
donkey.fun_fact: Donkeys are often more cautious than horses, which helps them avoid danger.

dragonfly.description: A fast flying insect with large eyes, two pairs of wings, and excellent aerial control.
dragonfly.habitat: Ponds, lakes, rivers, wetlands, and nearby vegetation.
dragonfly.diet: Carnivore: mosquitoes, flies, and other small insects.
dragonfly.fun_fact: Dragonflies can hover, fly backward, and turn sharply in the air.

duck.description: A waterbird with webbed feet and a broad bill, commonly seen on ponds and wetlands.
duck.habitat: Lakes, rivers, marshes, ponds, and coastal wetlands.
duck.diet: Omnivore: aquatic plants, seeds, insects, small fish, and crustaceans.
duck.fun_fact: A duck's waterproof feathers are maintained with oil from a gland near the tail.

eagle.description: A large bird of prey with strong talons, sharp vision, and powerful flight.
eagle.habitat: Mountains, forests, cliffs, wetlands, and open country.
eagle.diet: Carnivore: fish, birds, mammals, reptiles, and carrion.
eagle.fun_fact: Eagles can spot prey from great distances.

elephant.description: The largest land mammal, known for intelligence, social bonds, trunks, and tusks.
elephant.diet: Herbivore: grasses, bark, leaves, fruit, and roots.
elephant.fun_fact: Elephants use their trunks for breathing, smelling, drinking, touching, and grabbing.

flamingo.description: A tall wading bird with pink feathers, long legs, and a curved filter-feeding bill.
flamingo.habitat: Salt lakes, lagoons, mudflats, and shallow wetlands.
flamingo.diet: Algae, tiny crustaceans, and small aquatic organisms.
flamingo.fun_fact: Flamingos get their pink color from pigments in their food.

fly.description: A small winged insect with one pair of wings and quick flight reflexes.
fly.habitat: Homes, farms, forests, wetlands, and decaying organic matter.
fly.diet: Liquids, nectar, decaying matter, or blood depending on species.
fly.fun_fact: Flies process visual information very quickly, making them hard to swat.

fox.description: A small wild canine with a bushy tail, sharp senses, and flexible hunting behavior.
fox.habitat: Forests, grasslands, deserts, mountains, farms, and suburbs.
fox.diet: Omnivore: rodents, birds, insects, fruit, eggs, and carrion.
fox.fun_fact: Foxes often use sound and pouncing skills to hunt small prey under grass or snow.

goat.description: A hardy domesticated hoofed mammal known for climbing ability and browsing behavior.
goat.habitat: Farms, rocky hills, drylands, and pastures.
goat.diet: Herbivore: leaves, shrubs, grass, vines, and hay.
goat.fun_fact: Goats have rectangular pupils that help them scan wide areas.

goldfish.description: A small freshwater fish often kept in aquariums and ponds.
goldfish.habitat: Aquariums, ponds, and slow freshwater systems.
goldfish.diet: Omnivore: flakes, pellets, algae, plants, and tiny aquatic animals.
goldfish.fun_fact: Goldfish can recognize routines and learn simple feeding cues.

goose.description: A large waterbird known for strong pair bonds, loud calls, and seasonal migrations.
goose.habitat: Lakes, rivers, wetlands, fields, and parks.
goose.diet: Herbivore: grass, aquatic plants, grains, and seeds.
goose.fun_fact: Geese often fly in V-shaped formations to save energy.

gorilla.description: A large intelligent primate with strong social groups led by mature adults.
gorilla.diet: Mostly herbivore: leaves, stems, fruit, bark, and shoots.
gorilla.fun_fact: Gorillas communicate with gestures, vocal sounds, and body postures.

grasshopper.description: A jumping insect with powerful hind legs and chewing mouthparts.
grasshopper.habitat: Grasslands, fields, gardens, and crop areas.
grasshopper.fun_fact: Grasshoppers can leap many times their own body length.

hamster.description: A small burrowing rodent often kept as a pet, known for cheek pouches.
hamster.habitat: Dry grasslands, deserts, burrows, and homes as pets.
hamster.diet: Omnivore: seeds, grains, vegetables, fruit, and small insects.
hamster.fun_fact: Hamsters store food in expandable cheek pouches.

hare.description: A fast-running mammal related to rabbits, usually with longer legs and ears.
hare.habitat: Grasslands, open fields, tundra, deserts, and farmland.
hare.diet: Herbivore: grasses, herbs, bark, and twigs.
hare.fun_fact: Hares are born furred and with open eyes, unlike many rabbits.

hedgehog.description: A small mammal covered in protective spines and often active at night.
hedgehog.habitat: Gardens, hedgerows, forests, grasslands, and scrub.
hedgehog.diet: Omnivore: insects, worms, snails, fruit, and small animals.
hedgehog.fun_fact: Hedgehogs can roll into a tight ball when threatened.

hippopotamus.description: A massive semi-aquatic mammal with a broad mouth and strong territorial behavior.
hippopotamus.habitat: Rivers, lakes, wetlands, and nearby grasslands.
hippopotamus.fun_fact: Hippos spend much of the day in water to stay cool.

hornbill.description: A bird known for a large curved bill, often with a casque on top.
hornbill.diet: Omnivore: fruit, insects, small reptiles, and small mammals.
hornbill.fun_fact: Some hornbill females seal themselves inside tree cavities while nesting.

horse.description: A domesticated hoofed mammal known for speed, strength, and long partnership with humans.
horse.habitat: Farms, stables, grasslands, and managed pastures.
horse.diet: Herbivore: grass, hay, grains, and forage.
horse.fun_fact: Horses can sleep both standing up and lying down.

hummingbird.description: A tiny bird with rapid wingbeats and the ability to hover while feeding.
hummingbird.habitat: Forests, gardens, meadows, and tropical habitats.
hummingbird.fun_fact: Hummingbirds can beat their wings dozens of times per second.

hyena.description: A strong carnivorous mammal known for powerful jaws and complex social groups.
hyena.diet: Carnivore: hunted prey, carrion, and bones.
hyena.fun_fact: Hyenas can crush bones with their powerful jaws.

jellyfish.description: A soft-bodied aquatic animal with a bell-shaped body and trailing tentacles.
jellyfish.habitat: Oceans, coastal waters, and some brackish environments.
jellyfish.diet: Carnivore: plankton, fish eggs, larvae, and small marine animals.
jellyfish.fun_fact: Some jellyfish have existed in similar forms for hundreds of millions of years.

kangaroo.description: A marsupial with powerful hind legs, a long tail, and hopping movement.
kangaroo.habitat: Grasslands, woodlands, scrublands, and open plains.
kangaroo.fun_fact: Female kangaroos carry their young in a pouch.

koala.description: A tree-dwelling marsupial known for eating eucalyptus leaves and sleeping for long periods.
koala.fun_fact: Koalas have a specialized digestive system for tough eucalyptus leaves.

ladybugs.description: A small rounded beetle often recognized by bright red or orange wing covers with spots.
ladybugs.habitat: Gardens, fields, forests, and crop plants.
ladybugs.diet: Mostly aphids and other small plant pests.
ladybugs.fun_fact: Ladybugs help gardeners by eating aphids.

leopard.description: A spotted big cat known for stealth, strength, and excellent climbing ability.
leopard.habitat: Forests, savannas, mountains, grasslands, and scrublands.
leopard.diet: Carnivore: antelope, monkeys, birds, rodents, and other animals.
leopard.fun_fact: Leopards often carry prey into trees to keep it safe.

lion.description: A large social big cat that lives in groups called prides.
lion.habitat: Savannas, grasslands, scrublands, and open woodlands.
lion.fun_fact: Lions are among the most social of the big cats.

lizard.description: A reptile with scaly skin, usually four legs, and a wide range of body sizes.
lizard.habitat: Deserts, forests, grasslands, rocks, trees, and human areas.
lizard.diet: Mostly insects, plants, fruit, or small animals depending on species.
lizard.fun_fact: Some lizards can detach their tails to escape predators.

lobster.description: A marine crustacean with a hard shell, long antennae, and strong claws in many species.
lobster.habitat: Rocky sea floors, reefs, and coastal waters.
lobster.diet: Omnivore: fish, mollusks, algae, detritus, and small marine animals.
lobster.fun_fact: Lobsters grow by molting their hard outer shell.

mosquito.description: A small flying insect, with females of many species feeding on blood to produce eggs.
mosquito.habitat: Wetlands, ponds, puddles, forests, and urban areas with standing water.
mosquito.diet: Nectar; females of many species also feed on blood.
mosquito.fun_fact: Only female mosquitoes bite in many species.

moth.description: A winged insect related to butterflies, often active at night.
moth.habitat: Forests, gardens, fields, caves, and homes.
moth.diet: Nectar as adults; leaves, fabric fibers, or stored food as larvae depending on species.
moth.fun_fact: Many moths navigate using natural light from the moon and stars.

mouse.description: A small rodent with a pointed snout, large ears, and quick movements.
mouse.habitat: Fields, forests, farms, homes, and storage areas.
mouse.diet: Omnivore: seeds, grains, fruit, insects, and scraps.
mouse.fun_fact: Mice communicate using sounds, scents, and body language.

octopus.description: A highly intelligent marine mollusk with eight arms, soft body, and camouflage ability.
octopus.habitat: Coral reefs, rocky sea floors, tide pools, and deep ocean areas.
octopus.diet: Carnivore: crabs, shellfish, fish, and other marine animals.
octopus.fun_fact: Octopuses can change color and texture to blend into their surroundings.

okapi.description: A forest-dwelling hoofed mammal related to giraffes, with striped legs and a long tongue.
okapi.diet: Herbivore: leaves, buds, fruit, ferns, and fungi.
okapi.fun_fact: Okapis are close relatives of giraffes despite their zebra-like leg stripes.

orangutan.description: A highly intelligent great ape adapted for life in trees.
orangutan.diet: Mostly fruit, plus leaves, bark, insects, and eggs.
orangutan.fun_fact: Orangutans build new sleeping nests in trees.

otter.description: A playful semi-aquatic mammal with dense fur and strong swimming ability.
otter.habitat: Rivers, lakes, wetlands, coastal waters, and kelp forests.
otter.diet: Carnivore: fish, crustaceans, mollusks, frogs, and small animals.
otter.fun_fact: Some otters use rocks as tools to open shellfish.

owl.description: A nocturnal bird of prey with forward-facing eyes, silent flight, and sharp hearing.
owl.habitat: Forests, grasslands, deserts, barns, and urban edges.
owl.diet: Carnivore: rodents, birds, insects, reptiles, and fish.
owl.fun_fact: Many owls can fly almost silently because of special feather edges.

ox.description: A trained cattle animal traditionally used for pulling carts, plows, and heavy loads.
ox.habitat: Farms, fields, pastures, and rural work areas.
ox.diet: Herbivore: grass, hay, grains, and forage.
ox.fun_fact: Oxen are valued for strength, patience, and steady movement.

oyster.description: A shelled marine mollusk that filters water and often lives attached to hard surfaces.
oyster.habitat: Coastal waters, estuaries, reefs, and tidal zones.
oyster.diet: Microscopic plankton and particles filtered from water.
oyster.fun_fact: Oyster reefs can provide shelter for many other marine species.

panda.description: A black-and-white bear famous for eating bamboo and living in mountain forests.
panda.diet: Mostly bamboo, with occasional other plants or small animals.
panda.fun_fact: Pandas have a wrist bone that works like a thumb to grip bamboo.

parrot.description: A colorful intelligent bird with a strong curved beak and vocal learning ability.
parrot.habitat: Tropical forests, woodlands, savannas, and homes as pets.
parrot.diet: Seeds, fruit, nuts, flowers, nectar, and sometimes insects.
parrot.fun_fact: Some parrots can imitate sounds and human speech.

pelecaniformes.description: A group of water-associated birds that includes pelicans, herons, ibises, and related species.
pelecaniformes.habitat: Coasts, lakes, rivers, wetlands, marshes, and estuaries.
pelecaniformes.diet: Fish, amphibians, crustaceans, insects, and other aquatic animals.
pelecaniformes.fun_fact: Many pelecaniform birds are skilled hunters in shallow water.

penguin.description: A flightless seabird adapted for swimming with flipper-like wings.
penguin.habitat: Southern oceans, coasts, islands, and cold or temperate marine regions.
penguin.fun_fact: Penguins are excellent swimmers but cannot fly.

pig.description: A domesticated mammal with a strong snout, high intelligence, and flexible diet.
pig.habitat: Farms, barns, pastures, and managed pens.
pig.diet: Omnivore: grains, roots, vegetables, fruit, and prepared feed.
pig.fun_fact: Pigs are intelligent and can learn routines quickly.

pigeon.description: A common bird well adapted to cities, cliffs, farms, and human environments.
pigeon.habitat: Cities, farms, cliffs, bridges, parks, and open areas.
pigeon.diet: Seeds, grains, fruit, and food scraps.
pigeon.fun_fact: Pigeons have strong homing ability and can navigate over long distances.

porcupine.description: A rodent protected by sharp quills used as defense against predators.
porcupine.habitat: Forests, deserts, rocky areas, grasslands, and shrublands.
porcupine.diet: Herbivore: bark, leaves, twigs, roots, and fruit.
porcupine.fun_fact: Porcupine quills detach easily but are not shot out.

possum.description: A small to medium marsupial known for climbing and flexible feeding habits.
possum.habitat: Forests, suburbs, gardens, and tree hollows.
possum.diet: Omnivore: fruit, leaves, insects, eggs, small animals, and scraps.
possum.fun_fact: Some possums use their tails for balance while climbing.

raccoon.description: A clever nocturnal mammal with a masked face and highly sensitive front paws.
raccoon.habitat: Forests, wetlands, suburbs, cities, and river edges.
raccoon.diet: Omnivore: fruit, insects, eggs, fish, small animals, and scraps.
raccoon.fun_fact: Raccoons use their front paws to explore objects with great detail.

rat.description: A highly adaptable rodent found in many natural and urban environments.
rat.habitat: Cities, farms, sewers, forests, fields, and storage areas.
rat.diet: Omnivore: grains, fruit, insects, meat, and food waste.
rat.fun_fact: Rats are social animals and can learn routes and tasks quickly.

reindeer.description: A cold-adapted deer known for migration and antlers in both males and many females.
reindeer.habitat: Arctic tundra, boreal forests, and cold open landscapes.
reindeer.diet: Herbivore: lichens, mosses, grasses, leaves, and shrubs.
reindeer.fun_fact: Reindeer hooves change with the seasons to handle snow and soft ground.

rhinoceros.description: A large thick-skinned herbivore known for one or two horns and powerful body size.
rhinoceros.diet: Herbivore: grass, leaves, shoots, fruit, and branches.
rhinoceros.fun_fact: Rhino horns are made of keratin, the same material found in human hair and nails.

sandpiper.description: A small shorebird that probes mud or sand for tiny animals.
sandpiper.habitat: Beaches, mudflats, marshes, riverbanks, and wetlands.
sandpiper.diet: Insects, worms, small crustaceans, mollusks, and other tiny invertebrates.
sandpiper.fun_fact: Many sandpipers migrate very long distances each year.

seahorse.description: A small marine fish with a horse-like head, curled tail, and upright swimming posture.
seahorse.habitat: Seagrass beds, coral reefs, mangroves, and shallow coastal waters.
seahorse.fun_fact: Male seahorses carry developing young in a pouch.

seal.description: A marine mammal with flippers, streamlined body, and strong diving ability.
seal.habitat: Coastal waters, polar regions, rocky shores, and ice floes.
seal.diet: Carnivore: fish, squid, crustaceans, and other marine animals.
seal.fun_fact: Some seals can hold their breath for long dives.

shark.description: A cartilaginous fish with sharp senses, streamlined body, and many ecological roles.
shark.habitat: Oceans, reefs, coastal waters, open seas, and some rivers.
shark.diet: Carnivore: fish, seals, squid, rays, crustaceans, or plankton depending on species.
shark.fun_fact: Sharks can detect tiny electrical signals produced by other animals.

sheep.description: A domesticated grazing mammal raised for wool, milk, and meat.
sheep.diet: Herbivore: grass, hay, clover, and forage.
sheep.fun_fact: Sheep can recognize familiar faces in their flock.

snake.description: A legless reptile with a flexible body and diverse hunting strategies.
snake.habitat: Forests, deserts, grasslands, wetlands, farms, and water bodies.
snake.diet: Carnivore: rodents, birds, eggs, frogs, fish, insects, or other reptiles.
snake.fun_fact: Snakes smell by collecting scent particles with their tongues.

sparrow.description: A small seed-eating bird commonly found near people and open habitats.
sparrow.habitat: Cities, farms, gardens, grasslands, and shrublands.
sparrow.diet: Seeds, grains, insects, and small plant material.
sparrow.fun_fact: Sparrows often live close to humans and adapt well to urban life.

squid.description: A fast-swimming marine mollusk with arms, tentacles, and jet propulsion.
squid.habitat: Open ocean, coastal waters, reefs, and deep seas.
squid.diet: Carnivore: fish, shrimp, crustaceans, and other squid.
squid.fun_fact: Squid can release ink as a defense against predators.

squirrel.description: A small agile rodent known for climbing, jumping, and storing food.
squirrel.habitat: Forests, parks, gardens, urban trees, and grasslands.
squirrel.diet: Omnivore: nuts, seeds, fruit, fungi, insects, and buds.
squirrel.fun_fact: Squirrels often bury extra food and may forget some caches, helping trees spread.

starfish.description: A sea star with radial arms, tube feet, and strong regeneration ability in many species.
starfish.habitat: Tide pools, coral reefs, rocky shores, and sea floors.
starfish.diet: Carnivore: clams, mussels, small animals, and detritus.
starfish.fun_fact: Many sea stars can regrow lost arms.

swan.description: A large graceful waterbird with a long neck and strong pair bonds.
swan.habitat: Lakes, rivers, wetlands, ponds, and coastal waters.
swan.diet: Aquatic plants, algae, grasses, and small aquatic animals.
swan.fun_fact: Swans often form long-term pair bonds.

tiger.description: A powerful striped big cat and solitary hunter.
tiger.diet: Carnivore: deer, wild pigs, buffalo, and other medium to large prey.
tiger.fun_fact: Every tiger has a unique stripe pattern.

turkey.description: A large ground bird with a fan-shaped tail and distinctive calls.
turkey.habitat: Forests, grasslands, farms, and woodland edges.
turkey.diet: Omnivore: seeds, nuts, berries, insects, and small reptiles.
turkey.fun_fact: Wild turkeys can fly short distances despite their size.

turtle.description: A reptile with a protective shell and species adapted to land, freshwater, or sea.
turtle.habitat: Oceans, rivers, lakes, wetlands, forests, and grasslands.
turtle.diet: Plants, algae, insects, fish, jellyfish, or carrion depending on species.
turtle.fun_fact: A turtle's shell is part of its skeleton.

whale.description: A large marine mammal that breathes air and lives entirely in water.
whale.habitat: Oceans from polar waters to tropical seas.
whale.diet: Krill, plankton, fish, or squid depending on species.
whale.fun_fact: Some whales communicate with complex songs across long distances.

wolf.description: A social wild canine that hunts and travels in family groups called packs.
wolf.habitat: Forests, tundra, mountains, grasslands, and deserts.
wolf.diet: Carnivore: deer, elk, smaller mammals, carrion, and occasional fruit.
wolf.fun_fact: Wolf packs use howls to communicate across distance.

wombat.description: A burrowing marsupial with a compact body, strong claws, and slow steady movement.
wombat.diet: Herbivore: grasses, roots, bark, and sedges.

woodpecker.description: A tree-climbing bird known for drilling into wood with a strong beak.
woodpecker.habitat: Forests, woodlands, orchards, parks, and gardens.
woodpecker.diet: Insects, larvae, sap, fruit, nuts, and seeds.
woodpecker.fun_fact: Woodpeckers have shock-absorbing skull structures that help protect the brain.

zebra.description: A striped wild equine that lives in herds and grazes across open landscapes.
zebra.habitat: Savannas, grasslands, shrublands, and open woodlands.
zebra.diet: Herbivore: grasses, leaves, bark, and shrubs.
zebra.fun_fact: Each zebra has a unique stripe pattern.
